content.full_text:
State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance/Administration . . . Executive/Legal . . . Enforcement/Conflict of Interest
(916) 322-5660                                322-5901                           322-6441

June 9, 1976

Mr. Robert P. Will                                                    A-76-441
General Counsel
Metropolitan Water District of
Southern California
1111 Sunset Boulevard
Post Office Box 54153
Los Angeles, California  90054

Re:  Request for Extension of Time to File Conflict of Interest Code

Dear Mr. Will:

     As you state, the Metropolitan Water District of Southern
California has been directed to submit a Conflict of Interest Code
to the Fair Political Practices Commission on or before June 10,
1976.

     On behalf of the District, you have requested an extension of
time for the submission of a Conflict of Interest Code because the
provisions of the proposed Code are identical to Government Code
§87207(b) and, under certain circumstances, requires the disclosure
of clients of attorneys who are designated employees under the Code.
As you indicate, Government Code §87207(b) is the subject of liti-
gation with respect to client disclosure in the case of Hays v Wood
(No. 36319) in the Superior Court in the County of Mendocino. You
have asked that the submission date for the Conflict of Interest
Code of the Metropolitan Water District be extended to the fifteenth
day following a decision of the Superior Court in Hays v Wood in
order to comply with whatever decision is rendered by the Court.

     As you undoubtedly realize, virtually every Conflict of Interest
Code to be submitted to a code reviewing body pursuant to a deadline
date prior to the decision of the Superior Court in Mendocino County
would have to be delayed based upon the rationale of your request.
The Commission is cognizant of the pervasive uncertainty caused by
the issues raised in Hays v Wood. Accordingly, with respect to
public officials who must disclose pursuant to 87207(b), the Com-
mission will take no enforcement action against persons who fail
to file the names of clients pursuant to 87207(b) of the Political
Reform Act on the basis of Hays v Wood.

I can see this is a mostly blank document with what appears to be two small curved marks or symbols near the top center of the page, and possibly one or two other very faint marks elsewhere on the page. However, there is no clearly legible text content to transcribe from this image.

Robert P. Will
June 9, 1976
Page Two

The Fair Political Practices Commission, as the code reviewing
body for the Metropolitan Water District of Southern California is
also the filing officer for Statements of Economic Interests filed
pursuant to the Conflict of Interest Code of the district. As is
the case with public officials who statements filed Statements of Economic
Interests pursuant to Government Code §87200 et seq., the Commission
will take enforcement action against public officials who are
designated employees under a Conflict of Interest Code for which
it is the code reviewing body who fail to file the names of clients
pursuant to Conflict of Interest Code Sections based upon 87207(b)
until the final resolution of the issues presented in Davis v. Boyd.
On the basis of this representation and upon the necessity to
secure coverage of designated employees under a Conflict of Interest
Code, it appears unreasonable to grant your request for an extension
of time based upon the pending litigation. Accordingly, the request
of the Metropolitan Water District to submit its Conflict of Interest
Code at a date later than June 10, 1976, is denied.

If, on the basis of this staff decision, the District continues
to object to the submission of its Conflict of Interest Code on the
due date of June 10, 1976, please notify me by June 23, 1976, so that
the matter may be scheduled for Commission hearing on July 6, 7 or 8.

Sincerely,

Delbert L. Spurlock, Jr.
Chief, Conflicts of Interest
Division

/glb

I can see this is a mostly blank page with some very faint markings at the top (what appear to be two curved marks) and a few small marks scattered throughout, but no clearly legible text to transcribe.
